01 — The Conviction

India's home market is entering its defining decade

India produced its first D2C champions in fashion, beauty, and personal care — Sugar, Mamaearth, boAt, Noise. These brands were built on one insight: Indian consumers, armed with smartphones and rising incomes, wanted products that reflected their identity.

The second wave is here. And it is happening inside the home.

The Indian home and household market is on its way to USD 237 billion by 2030 (Deloitte). Organised retail is growing 34% year-on-year. Amazon's home category expands at 20% annually. Every metric points to the same thing: this is the right category, and right now is the right time.

The right time to build a home brand at scale was five years ago. The second-best time is right now — and Far Ocean is already in motion.

Far Ocean Brands Pvt Ltd, under its brands Laxis and Zolmix, is positioned exactly at this inflection. In just 18 months of operations, they crossed ₹24 crore in GMV — entirely on Amazon India, with zero offline presence. That is not traction. That is signal.

02 — The Problem

India's home shopper is underserved. Badly.

A couple in their late twenties settling into their first apartment — design-conscious, Instagram-native, with real disposable income — has almost nowhere to turn. The options are depressingly thin.

The old world
Traditional offline stores — slow-moving inventory, doesn't cater to Gen Z's pace.
Local banjara markets — same designs unchanged for a decade, low discovery.
Instagram shops — trend-reactive but narrow, no breadth, inconsistent quality.
What the market needs
Continuous NPD — new products that arrive before the customer knows she wants them.
Breadth with coherence — a full universe: storage, decor, kitchen, furnishing, gardening, lighting.
Value-for-money design — premium feel without the markup that gates the category.

Far Ocean's target customer — a 25–45 year old woman, annual home spend ₹20k–₹50k, buying across housewarmings, renovation cycles, festive seasons, and gifting — is impulse-driven, design-conscious, and digital-first. She has been systematically underserved by every incumbent.

03 — The Product

Six categories. Two brands. One obsession.

Far Ocean operates across six home categories under Laxis and Zolmix — home storage, home decor, home furnishing, kitchen and utility, gardening, and lighting. Every category choice is deliberate: high repurchase intent, high gifting velocity, customers willing to pay for design.

04 — The Market

A ₹2.5 lakh crore market in transition

The Indian home market is moving from unorganised to organised, offline to online, commodity to brand. Far Ocean sits at the intersection of all three shifts — and the numbers back it up.

Total Market
₹2.5L Cr
India Home & Household
Online Home D2C
₹35K Cr
Organised online segment
Serviceable
₹8K Cr
Design-led, mid-premium
  • USD 237B by 2030 — Deloitte's projection for India's home and household market, structural not cyclical.
  • 34% organised retail growth in FY23. Amazon home category growing 20% YoY. Distribution infrastructure building out fast.
  • 20% more real estate launches in metro cities in 2023. Every new home is a new shopping occasion.
  • Renovation cycles compressing 25% as families refresh faster. Repeat purchase frequency is structural.
05 — Traction

₹24 Crores. 18 months. Single channel.

Context matters: no quick commerce, no owned website, no offline presence. Every rupee of that ₹24 crore GMV came from Amazon India alone — built on product quality, search ranking, and genuine repeat purchasing.

"₹24 Cr in 18 months, entirely from Amazon, with Best Seller rankings and 10,000+ reviews. This is the foundation most D2C brands spend years trying to build."

06 — The Round

Vhelp Capital™ backed this conviction

Vhelp Capital™ invested in Far Ocean Brands as part of its pre-seed round — the first institutional capital into the business. We went in early because the signal was undeniable: a team that had already built ₹24 crores of GMV without a single rupee of external funding needed a partner who understood what that meant.

We invested because Far Ocean had already done the hardest thing in consumer D2C — found a product people genuinely love and a unit economics model that works. Everything from here is amplification.

The seed round now open — ₹2 crores — is being deployed to activate the next phase: multi-channel expansion, deeper SKU velocity, and brand-level marketing. Far Ocean has earned the right to scale. This capital is the accelerant.

  • Quick commerce launch — Blinkit, Zepto, Instamart activation, unlocking impulse D2C at city scale.
  • Owned D2C website — Building direct customer relationships, higher margin, brand equity.
  • SKU velocity & NPD — Doubling active SKUs during festive season, entering the home furnishing category with 100+ new products.
  • Brand infrastructure — Instagram flywheel, influencer ecosystem, content that turns a successful seller into a beloved brand.
07 — Roadmap FY 2026–27

Four quarters. One clear arc.

Apr – Jun
Multi-Channel Launch
Quick commerce and D2C website go live alongside Amazon.
Jul – Sep
Channel Scaling
Drive volume on new channels. Regional inventory clusters for 1-day delivery.
Oct – Dec
Festive NPD Sprint
2× SKU count. Seasonal and gifting collections. Peak sales activation.
Jan – Mar
Category Launch
Home furnishing enters with 100+ SKUs — highest AOV category in the portfolio.

08 — The Founder

One operator. Zero shortcuts.

Far Ocean was not started by a first-time founder hoping to figure out D2C. It was built by an experienced operator who understood supply chains, consumer behavior, and business fundamentals before writing the first product listing.

Himanshu Thakur
Co-Founder & CEO
Former consultant at Kearney — one of the world's top strategy firms. IIT Delhi alumnus with deep experience in consumer market strategy, operational design, and business transformation at Jubilant FoodWorks. Built ₹24 Cr in GMV from zero in 18 months without external capital.
Kearney IIT Delhi Jubilant FoodWorks
Vivek Kumar
Co-Founder & COO
Project Manager at Zeichenburo and former consultant at EDS. Brings hands-on operational expertise in supply chain management, vendor relationships, and the end-to-end product-to-fulfilment workflow that powers a home D2C brand at scale.
Zeichenburo EDS Consulting Supply Chain

The combination of Himanshu's strategic market insight and Vivek's operational discipline is the founding DNA that home D2C demands. This category wins on execution — and these two have already proved they can execute.

09 — Why Now

The window is open. It won't be forever.

India's home D2C category is at the same inflection that beauty and fashion were in 2018–2019. The brands that move decisively in the next 24 months will own this category for the next decade.

Far Ocean has the traction, the team, and the playbook. ₹24 crore in GMV from a single channel, in 18 months, without external capital — is the clearest early-stage signal we have seen in the home space.

We don't back pitches. We back proof. Far Ocean earned our conviction by building something real before asking for capital.

The product range — for every home
